title: Inbound email with suspicious sender domain
description: Google Workspace flagged an inbound message targeting helpdesk@corp.local from domain secure-signature.org. DMARC validation failed and the message body contained a URL with brand impersonation characteristics.

title: Sign-in from unrecognized geographic location
description: Okta detected a sign-in for l.melo from 179.21.43.17. The source IP geolocated to a different state than the user's baseline. Authentication completed using MFA from a device registered in Intune.

title: High-volume outbound data transfer detected
description: Veeam Backup & Replication reported a large data transfer from FS-FIN-01 to 172.18.40.21. Transfer volume exceeded the short-term rolling average. The job ran during the configured replication window.

title: PowerShell command line with bypass execution policy
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint flagged powershell.exe on IT-WS-042 invoked with -ExecutionPolicy Bypass parameter by user j.almeida via interactive session. Frequent in admin tooling; flagged for review.

title: PowerShell with encoded command argument
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected powershell.exe on DEV-WS-019 launched by user a.ferreira with -EncodedCommand (decoded length 412 bytes). Decoded body did not match known malicious patterns.

title: Impossible travel sign-in anomaly detected
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection flagged n.teixeira for impossible travel. A session shift was observed between IPs in different geographic regions, including 45.182.21.13. The sign-in used cached tokens via a GlobalProtect VPN tunnel.

title: Anomalous outbound data transfer volume
description: Darktrace flagged elevated outbound traffic from DR-SYNC-01 to 10.90.2.41. The transfer volume exceeded the 30-day baseline by 340 percent. The destination IP belongs to the disaster recovery subnet.

title: Backup job completed with warnings (Tier-2-Apps)
description: Veeam Backup & Replication finished the Tier-2-Apps backup job with 2 warnings: snapshot consolidation took longer than expected on VM-APP-DB-04 (32 minutes) and VM-APP-WEB-09 (28 minutes). Data protection SLA preserved.

title: wscript.exe spawning powershell.exe at user logon
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected wscript.exe on BR-WS-011 (user n.teixeira) executing logon.ps1 via cscript wrapper at user logon time. GPO logon script context.

title: TLS certificate expiring in 14 days for internal application
description: Qualys VMDR detected an upcoming expiration for the TLS certificate presented by app.corp.local (CN=app.corp.local). Expiration date: 2024-12-11. Owner: Application Platform team.

title: Unsigned binary execution on macOS endpoint
description: Jamf Protect detected an unsigned helper binary running on MAC-FIN-14 under user u.campos. The binary was launched from /usr/local/bin/ and made network connections to an internal distribution server.

title: Service enumeration and port scanning on production host
description: Qualys VMDR recorded scan activity from 10.10.5.20 targeting APP-SRV-12. Multiple TCP ports were probed including 445, 3389, and 5985. The source IP belongs to the vulnerability management VLAN.

title: File upload to cloud storage platform detected
description: Netskope identified r.nunes uploading files to onedrive.corp.local. The upload session involved multiple file transfers over HTTPS to a cloud storage endpoint.

title: PowerShell launching from non-standard directory
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint observed powershell.exe executing from C:\Users\Public\Tools\ on OPS-WS-007. Process invoked by scheduled task 'NightlyMaintenance' (user system).

title: File upload to cloud storage platform detected
description: Netskope identified g.oliveira uploading files to tenant-my.sharepoint.com. The upload session involved multiple file transfers over HTTPS to a cloud storage endpoint.

title: RDP and WinRM connection probing detected
description: Rapid7 InsightVM logged RDP and WinRM connection attempts from 10.10.8.31 toward DC-01. The probing targeted standard management ports 3389 and 5985.

title: Upload to unsanctioned cloud application
description: Netskope CASB blocked file upload by user m.castro to mega.nz (unsanctioned cloud storage category). File: 'project-backup.zip' (24 MB). Policy: 'Block-Personal-Cloud-Tier-2'.

title: Sign-in from unrecognized geographic location
description: Okta detected a sign-in for l.melo from 179.26.58.52. The source IP geolocated to a different state than the user's baseline. Authentication completed using MFA from a device registered in Intune.

title: Sign-in from unrecognized geographic location
description: Okta detected a sign-in for a.ferreira from 179.20.40.10. The source IP geolocated to a different state than the user's baseline. Authentication completed using MFA from a device registered in Intune.

title: Impossible travel sign-in anomaly detected
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection flagged d.araujo for impossible travel. A session shift was observed between IPs in different geographic regions, including 45.182.21.11. The sign-in used cached tokens via a GlobalProtect VPN tunnel.

title: Sign-in blocked by adaptive access policy
description: Okta blocked a sign-in attempt for a.ferreira from (ip); adaptive policy denied due to unmanaged device posture. User received guidance to enroll device in MDM.

title: Remote service creation matching PsExec pattern
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint observed service installation PSEXESVC.EXE on APP-SRV-12 from source 10.42.7.18 by user svc_admin. Routine remote ops by IT admins; correlate with change ticket.

title: Inbound email with suspicious sender domain
description: Google Workspace flagged an inbound message targeting legal@corp.local from domain vendor-update.info. DMARC validation failed and the message body contained a URL with brand impersonation characteristics.

title: Logon script executing encoded PowerShell
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint flagged BR-WS-012 logon script vpn_bootstrap.ps1 executing with -EncodedCommand argument under user p.freitas at user logon. Origin: SYSVOL distribution.

title: Inbound connection blocked by sanctioned-country geo-rule
description: Cisco Secure Firewall blocked an inbound TCP/443 connection from 203.0.113.42 (geolocated to a sanctioned country) targeting the external load balancer. Action: drop. Threat score: low.

title: Internal network port scanning from corporate subnet
description: Cisco Secure Firewall logged Nessus-SCAN-01 probing multiple internal ports from source IP 10.60.10.15. The scan targeted management interfaces across three VLANs. The source system is registered in the vulnerability management VLAN.

title: VBScript spawning cmd.exe from logon GPO
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected drives.vbs on BR-WS-013 spawning cmd.exe at logon under user d.araujo. Logon script registered via Group Policy 'Branch-Drives-Map'.

title: WMI process creation on remote host
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected wmiprvse.exe spawning powershell.exe on DB-SQL-04 invoked from 10.40.5.22 (user svc_dba). Remote PowerShell execution via WMI.

title: PowerShell process started with hidden window
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected powershell.exe on FIN-WS-203 launched with -WindowStyle Hidden by user m.lima. Common in admin scripts; correlated with no follow-up suspicious activity.

title: Internal network port scanning from corporate subnet
description: Cisco Secure Firewall logged Rapid7-SCAN-02 probing multiple internal ports from source IP 10.60.10.18. The scan targeted management interfaces across three VLANs. The source system is registered in the vulnerability management VLAN.

title: Repeated MFA push prompts within short window
description: Okta detected 12 MFA push notifications sent to user j.gomes within 4 minutes from IP 187.45.10.33. Possible MFA fatigue attempt or user device issue; sign-in not completed.

title: Deviation from peer group baseline traffic profile
description: Darktrace flagged host ENG-WS-203 (user a.lima) as deviating from engineering peer group baseline: 4.2x normal outbound volume to GitHub (legitimate code push session, but flagged for review).

title: PowerShell with download cradle pattern
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint flagged powershell.exe on MKT-WS-088 invoking IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString to internal repo at https://artifacts.corp.local/installer.ps1 by user d.azevedo.

title: Notarization check failure on executed binary
description: Jamf Protect detected execution of binary /usr/local/bin/dev_helper on MAC-DEV-08 by user r.tavares. Binary failed Apple notarization check (Gatekeeper assessment: source not trusted). Allowed by user override.

title: Privileged action by service principal
description: Okta logged an administrative action (App assignment update) performed by service principal okta-terraform-runner on application 'Salesforce'. Action initiated from CI/CD pipeline IP 10.10.5.42.

title: Impossible travel sign-in anomaly detected
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection flagged k.barbosa for impossible travel. A session shift was observed between IPs in different geographic regions, including 45.182.21.12. The sign-in used cached tokens via a GlobalProtect VPN tunnel.

title: Suspicious sequence of script hosts at logon
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint observed wscript.exe → cscript.exe → powershell.exe chain on BR-WS-014 by user k.barbosa during logon initialization. Multi-host pattern, no malicious indicators.

title: Self-service password reset request rate-limited
description: Okta SSPR system rate-limited password reset attempts for user c.ramos after 5 attempts within 10 minutes. Sign-in flow paused.

title: PowerShell remoting (WinRM) inbound execution
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint observed wsmprovhost.exe on ENG-SRV-04 hosting an inbound PowerShell remoting session from 10.40.5.20 (user svc_deploy). Build/deploy automation pattern.

title: Container image vulnerability detected
description: Rapid7 InsightVM scan of container image 'corp-registry/api-gateway:v3.4.1' identified CVE-2024-21626 (runc escape, CVSS 8.6) in base layer. Image deployed across 14 production pods.

title: Email failed DMARC and SPF authentication
description: Google Workspace Security blocked inbound email from {domain} addressed to multiple recipients. DMARC: fail (p=reject); SPF: softfail. Subject: 'Updated invoice'. Quarantined.

title: First-seen external destination for managed device
description: Darktrace identified host MKT-WS-088 establishing connection to never-before-seen external destination (cdn.adfreshtech.io). Investigation: marketing automation platform recently onboarded; expected.

title: High-volume outbound data transfer detected
description: Veeam Backup & Replication reported a large incremental backup from FS-FIN-01 to 172.18.40.21. Transfer volume exceeded the rolling average by 180 percent.

title: Risky OAuth grant detected for user account
description: Netskope detected user s.souza granted OAuth permissions to third-party app 'EmailScannerPro' (publisher unverified) on Microsoft 365 account. Scopes requested: Mail.Read, Files.Read.All. Risk score: high.

title: DCOM-based lateral movement (MMC20.Application)
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected mmc.exe spawning cmd.exe on OPS-SRV-09 via DCOM activation from 10.40.5.18. MMC20.Application object instantiated remotely by user svc_monitor.

title: Sign-in from anonymous IP address (Tor)
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection flagged sign-in for user d.araujo originating from anonymized IP 45.182.21.11 (Tor exit node). Risk level: medium. User MFA challenge completed successfully.

title: Risk-based step-up authentication required
description: Okta evaluated sign-in for user b.smith from IP 179.20.88.15 as medium risk (new device + new location). Step-up authentication challenge issued; user completed second factor.

title: Token replay detected
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection detected reuse of refresh token for user p.freitas from two distinct IPs (200.150.30.5 and previously-seen 10.40.1.12) within 90 seconds. Possible session hijack or load-balancer artifact.

title: Bulk OAuth token revocation for SaaS application
description: Okta administrator revoked active OAuth refresh tokens for the Salesforce application (28 sessions invalidated). Action initiated from Okta admin console by user iam-admin@corp.local.

title: Logon script writing to startup folder
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected printer_map.vbs on BR-WS-015 (user v.cardoso) writing to %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup at logon. Persistence-like pattern flagged.

title: wscript.exe with encoded JScript at logon
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint flagged wscript.exe on BR-WS-011 executing teams_cleanup.ps1 wrapper containing encoded JScript payload at logon under n.teixeira.

title: Unfamiliar sign-in properties
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection flagged sign-in for user k.barbosa from IP 45.182.21.12 as unfamiliar (new device + new location + new ISP). Risk level: low. User identity verified via MFA.

title: Personal account use blocked by SaaS policy
description: Netskope blocked sign-in attempt by user f.lima to Dropbox using personal email (bsmith.personal@gmail.com) from corporate device. Tenant restriction policy enforced.

title: Asset out-of-scope: unmanaged host discovered
description: Rapid7 InsightVM discovery scan identified host 10.42.50.137 (hostname: dev-jenkins-old) responding to SNMP queries. Host not in managed asset inventory; possible shadow IT.

title: Anomalous east-west traffic pattern detected
description: Cisco Secure Firewall flagged unusual lateral connection volume from 10.42.18.51 to 23 distinct internal hosts on TCP/445 within 6 minutes. Behavior deviates from baseline for source segment.

title: End-of-life software detected
description: Qualys VMDR identified end-of-life software on WEB-PRD-07: OpenSSL 1.0.2 (EOL since 2019-12-31). Vendor support discontinued; upgrade to OpenSSL 3.x recommended.

title: Anonymous SMB authentication followed by service creation
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint flagged service creation on BACKUP-SRV-02 from anonymous SMB session originating at 172.18.40.21. Routine Veeam DR replication pattern; correlate with backup window.

title: Malicious attachment detected by sandbox analysis
description: Google Workspace Security sandboxed attachment 'OrderConfirmation_2024.docm' from sender external@unknown-llc.tld and detected macro execution attempting to download payload. Quarantined.

title: Insecure default configuration on application server
description: Rapid7 InsightVM identified default credentials accepted on Tomcat manager interface at app-srv-12.corp.local:8080. Configuration deviates from security baseline.

title: Password spray attempt blocked
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection detected 47 failed authentications across 32 distinct user accounts originating from IP 200.150.30.88 within 8 minutes. Pattern matches password spray. Smart lockout engaged.

title: Insecure TLS cipher suite advertised on internal service
description: Qualys VMDR detected TLS 1.0 enabled with RC4-SHA cipher on app.corp.local:8443. Internal service exposed to BEAST/CRIME attack vectors. Recommended: disable TLS 1.0/1.1 and weak ciphers.

title: User flagged as high risk
description: Microsoft Entra ID Protection elevated risk level for user p.freitas to High after multiple anomaly signals (atypical location, leaked credentials match, anonymous IP). Account flagged for IT review.

title: Encrypted traffic with weak TLS cipher
description: Cisco Secure Firewall observed TLS 1.0 with RC4 cipher in use between 10.40.7.18 and 192.168.50.9:8443. Internal application not enforcing modern TLS. Compliance policy flagged.

title: New device claim for existing user
description: Okta recorded device enrollment for user c.ramos: new Windows device (claimed via Okta Verify) replacing previous registered device. Device serial: WV-XXXX-9821.

title: New high-severity CVE detected on finance workstation
description: Rapid7 InsightVM identified CVE-2024-30040 (Microsoft MSHTML platform spoofing, CVSS 7.8) on FIN-WS-200 during scheduled vulnerability scan. Patch available via Patch Tuesday April 2024.

title: High-volume outbound data transfer detected
description: Veeam Backup & Replication reported a large data transfer from VM-ERP-02 to 172.18.40.22. Transfer volume exceeded the short-term rolling average. The job ran during the configured replication window.

title: DNS query to sinkholed domain (legacy malware family)
description: Cisco Umbrella blocked an outbound DNS query from IT-LT-007 to ek-traffic-stats[.]com which resolves to a Microsoft sinkhole. Indicates legacy adware on the host. Security category: Sinkhole.

title: Reply-chain hijack attempt detected
description: Google Workspace Security flagged reply email referencing existing thread ID with previously-unseen sender (acme-payables-billing@spoofed-domain.tld). Reply-chain hijack pattern detected.

title: CIS benchmark policy failure on Windows host
description: Qualys Policy Compliance flagged failure on CIS Benchmark Windows Server 2022 v2.0 control 18.9.59.3.9.5: 'Ensure UAC is configured to behavior of elevation prompt'. Host: DB-SQL-04.

title: PowerShell launched by uncommon parent process
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint observed powershell.exe on ENG-WS-145 spawned by msbuild.exe (build agent context) by service account svc_buildbot. First occurrence on this host.
